identity.short_name: GCD identities for exponential sequences - Buffed Seed

problem_spec.summary: Define a gcd-of-exponentials sequence from two affine exponent progressions. Under tight congruence and coprimality guards, the first index at which an odd prime power divides the gcd is forced. Solving it requires an ORDER step (multiplicative order modulo a prime power) and a LIN step (Bezout/Euclid reduction of a gcd of linear forms to a solvable congruence).
givens: $p$ is an odd prime and $k\ge 2$; set $M=p^k$.
ask:
\
        Show that the set $\{t\ge 1:\ p^k\mid G_t\}$ is nonempty and that, under the stated
        invariants,
        \[
          p^k\mid G_t \iff \mathrm{ord}_{p^k}(a)\mid \gcd(mt+r,\ nt+s)\iff p^{k-1}\mid (mt+r).
        \]

\
        Let $t_0$ be the least positive integer $t$ such that $p^k\mid G_t$.
        Report the final answer $t_0 \bmod 1000$.

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: selects the prime whose prime-power modulus forces a nontrivial order regime

parameter(s).name: k
parameter(s).purpose: sets the prime-power modulus $M=p^k$ (non-prime, order not trivial)

parameter(s).name: a
parameter(s).purpose: fixes a base with forced order behavior modulo $p^k$

parameter(s).name: m
parameter(s).purpose: slope of the first exponent progression; invertible modulo $p^{k-1}$ to force a unique minimal index

parameter(s).name: n
parameter(s).purpose: slope of the second exponent progression; participates in the determinant $D=ms-nr$

parameter(s).name: r
parameter(s).purpose: offset of the first exponent progression; prevents the $t=0$ shortcut and controls the extremal index

parameter(s).name: s
parameter(s).purpose: offset of the second exponent progression; couples with $(m,n,r)$ through $D=ms-nr$

invariant(s).name: forced_order_prime_power_divisor_and_nondivisor
invariant(s).statement:
\
        With $p$ odd, $k\ge 2$, and $a\equiv 1+p\pmod{p^k}$, the multiplicative order satisfies
        $\mathrm{ord}_{p^k}(a)=p^{k-1}$. In particular, the order has the forced nontrivial divisor $p$
        and is not divisible by any prime factor of $(p-1)$ (it is a pure $p$-power).

invariant(s).name: determinant_Bezout_reduction_of_linear_exponent_gcd
invariant(s).statement:
\
        Let $D=ms-nr$. For every $t\ge 1$, any common divisor of $(mt+r)$ and $(nt+s)$ divides $D$.
        Under the guard $\gcd(m,D)=1$, this sharpens to
        $\gcd(mt+r,\ nt+s)=\gcd(mt+r,\ D)$ for all $t\ge 1$.

invariant(s).name: existence_guard_for_order_divisibility
invariant(s).statement: The condition $p^{k-1}\mid D$ (with $D=ms-nr\neq 0$) is necessary and sufficient (under $\gcd(m,p)=1$) for the existence of some $t\ge 1$ with $p^k\mid G_t$.

invariant(s).name: anti_triviality_guard_excludes_t_equals_1
invariant(s).statement: The constraint $p^{k-1}\nmid(m+r)$ ensures $t_0>1$, ruling out the immediate first-term solution.

essential_assumption(s): The exponents are affine functions of the index $t$ and are coupled through $D=ms-nr$; this prevents the problem from collapsing to the one-step template $\gcd(a^m-1,a^n-1)=a^{\gcd(m,n)}-1$ and forces a second phase (Bezout/linear congruence) after the order reduction.

The forced-order hypothesis $a\equiv 1+p\pmod{p^k}$ is essential: without a controlled value of $\mathrm{ord}_{p^k}(a)$, the divisibility condition $p^k\mid G_t$ does not reduce to a unique solvable congruence for $t$.
uniqueness_certificate(s): Under $\gcd(m,p)=1$, the congruence $mt\equiv -r\pmod{p^{k-1}}$ has a unique solution class modulo $p^{k-1}$, hence a unique least positive solution $t_0$. The reported output is then uniquely determined as $t_0 \bmod 1000$ by the normalization rule.

technique(s).name: Euclidean_algorithm_on_exponents
technique(s).purpose: derive $\gcd(a^u-1,a^v-1)=a^{\gcd(u,v)}-1$ by iterating Euclid steps on $(u,v)$
outputs: reduction of the exponential gcd to a gcd of exponents

technique(s).name: Bezout_on_linear_exponents
technique(s).purpose: use Bezout linear combinations to show common divisors divide $D=ms-nr$, then sharpen to $\gcd(mt+r,nt+s)=\gcd(mt+r,D)$ under $\gcd(m,D)=1$

technique(s).name: order_arguments_mod_prime_power
technique(s).purpose: characterize $p^k\mid a^t-1$ via $\mathrm{ord}_{p^k}(a)\mid t$ and compute $\mathrm{ord}_{p^k}(a)$ from $a\equiv 1+p\pmod{p^k}$

technique(s).name: order_to_Bezout_bridge
technique(s).purpose: link the ORDER step ($\mathrm{ord}_{p^k}(a)\mid\gcd(mt+r,nt+s)$) with the LIN step (Bezout reduction of $\gcd(mt+r,nt+s)$ to a linear congruence in $t$)
outputs: a single congruence $mt\equiv -r\pmod{p^{k-1}}$ whose least positive solution is $t_0$

theorem(s).name: gcd(a,b)=gcd(a,b-ka)
theorem(s).role: Euclid step used to transfer gcd invariance

theorem(s).name: gcd(a,b)=gcd(a, b_mod_a)
theorem(s).role: remainder form of the Euclid step, transferred to exponents

theorem(s).name: gcd(a^u-1,a^v-1)=a^{gcd(u,v)}-1
theorem(s).role: closed-form gcd identity from Euclid on exponents

theorem(s).name: linear_congruence_uniqueness_when_invertible
theorem(s).role: if $\gcd(m,p)=1$ then $mt\equiv -r\pmod{p^{k-1}}$ has a unique residue class modulo $p^{k-1}$

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Divisibility $M\mid a^t-1$ is controlled by periodicity of $a^t$ modulo $M$ (multiplicative order).
Any common divisor of two integers divides any integer linear combination (Bezout), enabling determinant reductions for gcds of linear forms.

critical_trick(s): Use Euclid on exponents to rewrite $\gcd(a^{mt+r}-1,a^{nt+s}-1)$ as $a^{\gcd(mt+r,nt+s)}-1$.
Compute/force $\mathrm{ord}_{p^k}(a)=p^{k-1}$ from $a\equiv 1+p\pmod{p^k}$, turning $p^k$-divisibility into the exponent condition $p^{k-1}\mid\gcd(mt+r,nt+s)$.

(Interaction kernel) Apply Bezout with the determinant $D=ms-nr$ to reduce $\gcd(mt+r,nt+s)$ to a single linear congruence in $t$, then take the least positive solution and normalize it modulo $1000$.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: Core integer arithmetic: gcd, lcm, modular exponentiation scaffolding.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: Optional symbolic/number theory utilities: factorint, primerange, mod_inverse, crt; useful for verification tasks.

import_hint(s): from sympy import factorint, crt, mod_inverse

task(s).name: structural_sanity_check_small_bounds
task(s).reason: For small admissible tuples $(p,k,a,m,n,r,s)$, brute-force compute $G_t$ for $1\le t\le p^{k-1}$ and confirm $p^k\mid G_t \iff p^{k-1}\mid (mt+r)$ and the minimality of $t_0$.

task(s).name: order_verification_prime_power
task(s).reason: Verify numerically that $\mathrm{ord}_{p^k}(a)=p^{k-1}$ for sample admissible parameters (compute the smallest $t$ with $a^t\equiv 1\pmod{p^k}$).
